sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Kendrick Traction Splint is a compact traction device for selected isolated femoral shaft fractures. Its smaller footprint can make it useful in confined spaces or retrieval environments where a bulkier device is less practical.

sections.title: Application
sections.content:
1. Confirm that traction splinting is appropriate and provide analgesia first

2. Size the device against the uninjured leg and position the proximal support correctly

3. Secure the ankle hitch and connect the traction mechanism

4. Apply steady traction until pain and alignment improve, then secure the remaining straps

5. Reassess distal neurovascular status and ongoing comfort

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Apply the same general traction-splint indications and contraindications used for other femoral traction devices
- Compact design can help in confined spaces, but only if the injury pattern is suitable
- Reassess traction, strap security, and neurovascular findings during movement and transport
